session.name: MPI-283 frame-accurate video player (mediabunny hybrid) — steps 1-4 + color fix

goal.original: Frame-accurate video player via mediabunny (hybrid): native <video> for PLAY, mediabunny WebCodecs exact-frame canvas overlay for paused/frame-step/trim. Must work on Mac/Linux/Windows with 100% certainty.
goal.summary: Steps 1-4 done + user-verified (frame-step exact, sub-range loop, color match). A significant COLOR bug was found and fixed (WebCodecs mis-converts YUV matrix vs <video>). Remaining: step 5 (TrimBar), step 6 (cleanup+docs), plus 3 user-noted follow-ups and 1 follow-up card.

completed: 1. mediabunny@1.50.8 dep + smoke (canDecode, 20/20 distinct exact frames)
2. js/services/frameSink.js (VideoSampleSink + MANUAL YUV->RGB color-matched to <video>; canDecode gate + native fallback)
3. MpiVideoSurface canvas overlay + frameSink-backed frameStep (frame-step exact, USER-VERIFIED; overlay-size fix; scrub-back-glitch reorder fix pending re-eyeball)
4. MpiVideoControlBar sub-range loop freeze fix (time-based boundary + re-play on wrap; deleted _effectiveFps; in!=0 pin fix). Auto-passed; user eyeball pending
pending: 5. MpiTrimBar — playhead/in/out map to exact frame timestamps; keep _snap end-stick; ALSO fix Wan last-frame-unreachable (frame 33 shows in scrub but can't step to it)
6. Cleanup + docs — remove dead seek-math; video-player subsystem doc (audio-clock/PTS root cause + hybrid contract + color matrix rule); ASK before .claude/rules edit
FOLLOW-UP CARD: apply the same BT.601/709 matrix fix to the extend-from-last-frame EXTRACTION path (that recolor caused the transition color drift the user saw)

completed: checklist.md fully updated with per-step results + color reference + open findings
plan.md has Cross-platform guarantee section (canDecode gate) + color notes
pending: Step 6 will write the video-player subsystem doc (docs/) — audio-clock/PTS root cause, hybrid contract, and the color matrix rule (height>=720->BT.709 else BT.601 limited; WebCodecs mis-reports colorSpace.matrix, don't trust it)
Create the extend-from-last-frame extraction color follow-up card
Doc-drift: MpiVideoSurface gained a canvas overlay + frameSink dep; MpiVideoControlBar loop logic changed — ASK user before updating .claude/rules component maps (CLAUDE.md doc-drift rule)

next_action.description: Get user eyeball on the still-pending visual items first (in!=0 sub-range loop after the pin fix; scrub-back glitch after the paint-before-seek reorder). Then implement step 5 (MpiTrimBar exact-frame mapping + fix Wan last-frame-unreachable). Then step 6 (cleanup + docs). Then create the extend-from-last-frame color follow-up card.

key_decisions: Cross-platform guarantee is by CONSTRUCTION: every sink gated on track.canDecode(); false/error -> return null -> caller keeps native <video>. Never worse than today on Mac/Linux/Windows. Proven live (bogus url -> null, no throw).
MpiSaveVideo left UNTOUCHED (user decision) — it's already libx264/yuv420p software h264 (GPU-agnostic), the Mac-safe path. Crop-export (our ffmpeg) confirmed color-clean vs source in DaVinci.

COLOR: WebCodecs canvas decode mis-converts YUV->RGB (mis-tags matrix as bt709 even for BT.601 SD content, renders vivid; crbug 40539111/343011434). copyTo/drawImage/createImageBitmap ALL give the same mis-converted pixels (empirically disproven the research's copyTo fix). ONLY exact fix = manual YUV(I420)->RGB from raw planes. Matrix by height: >=720 BT.709, else BT.601, limited range (matches libx264 tag + Chromium <video>). Verified pixel-near-perfect vs <video> (SD resid 0.12, HD resid 0.59). DaVinci/<video>/our-ffmpeg-export = color truth.

Frame-step: keep integer frame math, drop the 0.25*fs currentTime bias; canvas owns visible pixels while stepping; set video.currentTime AFTER painting canvas (for audio/PLAY sync + to avoid native seek flashing).

Sub-range loop: drive boundary off native time vs out-point directly (half-frame lead), NOT reverse-engineered frame index (that rounding froze it). Only the out-point matters during playback (time moves forward); play-start in-seek handled by _seekRangeStartIfNeeded.
constraints: frameSink imports mediabunny via '/node_modules/mediabunny/dist/bundles/mediabunny.mjs' (express.static(__dirname) serves it; runtime dep; portable build copies node_modules). Pure JS, no per-OS binary.
Media serves via /project-file?path= (same-origin, range 206). UrlSource works directly, no BlobSource needed.
frameSink only hand-converts I420/I420A (our libx264 output). Other formats -> return null -> native fallback.
Color cost is PER-DISPLAYED-FRAME only (paused/step), so clip length is irrelevant (30s / 3-min combines fine). Resolution-dependent per-pixel loop, one frame at a time.

TEST HARNESS: drive live app at http://127.0.0.1:3000 via Playwright chromium (installed). WebCodecs needs Chromium/Electron, NOT bare node. Chromium 147 WebCodecs is a superset of Electron 41's ~140. IMPORTANT: write test scripts with the Write tool, NOT bash heredoc — heredoc collapses \\ in Windows paths (C:\\Users -> C:Users) and silently 404s.
LTX frame0==frame1 = NON-BUG (DaVinci-confirmed content, Wan/LTX 48+1 pad). User may drop frame0 in workflow. No player change.

Wan last-frame (frame 33 @16fps 33-frame clip): shows in scrub bar but can't STEP to it. User says minor. REVISIT step 5 — likely lastFrame=_frameCount-1 vs round(hiSec*eff) boundary math; verify frame count vs DaVinci.

resume_prompt: You are resuming MPI-283 'Frame-accurate video player via mediabunny (hybrid)' on branch 1.2.0. Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/90da2b66-227c-48d0-b032-881e588e0a4e.json and .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-283/checklist.md, then run mpi-continue MPI-283. Steps 1-4 are done and user-verified: frame-accurate stepping via a mediabunny WebCodecs canvas overlay (js/services/frameSink.js) over the native <video>, sub-range loop fixes in MpiVideoControlBar, and a MAJOR color fix — WebCodecs mis-converts the YUV->RGB matrix (renders vivid vs the correct <video>/DaVinci), fixed by manual YUV(I420)->RGB in frameSink choosing BT.709 for height>=720 else BT.601 (limited range). Cross-platform is guaranteed by construction (every sink gated on track.canDecode(); null -> native <video> fallback). All testing is done by driving the live app at http://127.0.0.1:3000 via Playwright chromium (write scripts with the Write tool, never bash heredoc — it mangles Windows backslash paths). NEXT: first get the user's eyeball on the two still-pending visual fixes (in!=0 sub-range loop after the playhead-pin fix; scrub-back interpolated-flash after the paint-before-seek reorder). Then do step 5 (MpiTrimBar exact-frame mapping + investigate Wan's unreachable last frame) and step 6 (remove dead seek-math + write a video-player subsystem doc capturing the audio-clock/PTS root cause + the color matrix rule; ASK before editing .claude/rules). Also create a follow-up card to apply the same BT.601/709 matrix fix to the extend-from-last-frame EXTRACTION path (that recolor is what drifted transition colors). Nothing is committed yet — commit is mpi-end's job.
